title: Add more playable and enemy characters to the 3D fighting game

requirements.summary: Extend the Character interface and level configuration types to support multiple character types with a characterType field
acceptanceCriteria: The Character interface includes a 'characterType' field that identifies the character variant.
LEVEL_CONFIGS or equivalent constants specify enemy character types per level.
The TypeScript types compile without errors.
Character3D.tsx uses the characterType field to render the correct mesh for each character.

file_operations.description: Add a 'characterType' string field to the Character interface to differentiate between character variants (e.g., 'ug', 'newPlayer', 'sukuna', 'newEnemy1', 'newEnemy2'). Extend the LEVEL_CONFIGS structure to include an 'enemyTypes' array that specifies which enemy character types appear in each level. Add a new CharacterStats type that defines health, speed, attackDamage, and attackRange for each character type. Create CHARACTER_STATS constant mapping each characterType to its stats.

requirements.summary: Add at least 2 new distinct enemy character types beyond Sukuna to the 3D fighting game
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 new enemy character types are implemented with unique names (e.g., not 'Sukuna').
Each new enemy has a visually distinct 3D mesh with its own color scheme and materials.
Each new enemy has unique stats (health, speed, damage) defined in the game type/config.
New enemies appear in the game during gameplay (in later levels or mixed with existing enemies).
New enemies use the existing enemy AI logic (moving toward UG, attacking at close range).
Health bars in the HUD correctly reflect the active enemy's name and health.

file_operations.description: Add two new enemy character rendering variants with distinct 3D meshes. Create 'Gojo' enemy using cyan/blue color scheme with white emissive highlights and a taller, slimmer body structure. Create 'Toji' enemy using dark green/black color scheme with red emissive highlights and a bulkier body structure. Update the character rendering logic to check the character.characterType field and render the appropriate mesh (UG, Sukuna, Gojo, or Toji) with their unique materials and colors. Each new enemy should use grouped meshes similar to the existing Sukuna structure.

Update enemy creation logic to use the characterType field from LEVEL_CONFIGS. When spawning enemies, assign the appropriate characterType from the level configuration's enemyTypes array. Ensure enemy stats (health, speed, attackDamage) are applied from CHARACTER_STATS based on the enemy's characterType. Update level progression logic to spawn enemies with the character types specified in LEVEL_CONFIGS for each level.

Update enemy AI logic to use character-specific stats from the character.characterType field. Ensure attack range, speed, and damage values are read from the character's assigned stats rather than hardcoded values. The AI behavior (movement toward player, attack at close range) should work consistently for all enemy types.

Update the HUD to display the active enemy's name based on their characterType field. Map characterType strings to display names (e.g., 'sukuna' → 'Sukuna', 'gojo' → 'Gojo', 'toji' → 'Toji'). Ensure the enemy health bar correctly reflects the current enemy's name and health percentage.

requirements.summary: Add at least 1 new playable character option with a character selection screen before the game starts
acceptanceCriteria: A character selection screen is displayed before the game begins with at least 2 playable characters (UG + at least 1 new character).
Each playable character has a unique name and visually distinct 3D mesh.
Each playable character has distinct base stats (health, speed, attack power) in the game types.
The selected character is used as the player character throughout the game session.
The HUD correctly displays the selected player character's name and health bar.
All existing controls (keyboard + on-screen buttons) function correctly for the new playable character(s).

file_operations.description: Create a character selection screen component that displays before the game starts. Show at least 2 playable character options: UG (original) and a new character named 'Yuji'. Display each character option with their name, a visual preview (colored box or simple 3D preview), and their base stats (health, speed, attack power). Provide a 'Select' button for each character. When a character is selected, call an onCharacterSelected callback passing the selected characterType ('ug' or 'yuji'). Style the screen with dark theme consistent with the existing game UI.

Add a new playable character rendering variant for 'Yuji' with a distinct 3D mesh using red/orange color scheme with yellow emissive highlights and a medium-build body structure. Update the character rendering logic to handle the 'yuji' characterType alongside 'ug'. Ensure the sword mesh and attack animations work correctly for both playable character types.

Add state to track whether the character selection phase is active and which playable character was selected. Initially show the CharacterSelectScreen component before starting the game. When a character is selected via the callback, store the selected characterType and transition to the game. Update player character creation to use the selected characterType and apply the corresponding stats from CHARACTER_STATS. Ensure the selected character persists throughout the entire game session.

Update the HUD to display the player character's name based on their characterType field. Map characterType strings to display names (e.g., 'ug' → 'UG', 'yuji' → 'Yuji'). Ensure the player health bar shows the correct character name throughout the game.

Update the header to display 'UG vs SUKUNA' as the default title, but allow it to reflect the selected playable character if a new character is chosen (e.g., 'YUJI vs SUKUNA'). This can be achieved by passing the selected player characterType as a prop to the header or by managing it in App state.
